items.evidence: Earlier in the same Mary Jenkins transcript, she identifies Rev. James Grant as 'my pastor' and names the first Albany Movement mass meeting venue as 'the Mount Zion Baptist Church under Rev. James Grant.' Rev. Dr. E. James Grant served as pastor of Mount Zion Baptist Church in Albany, Georgia from 1944 to 1991 (a 47-year tenure documented by Mt. Zion's own published history). The passage in question describes Rev. James Grant having 'just finished the sermon for that Sunday' — a regular Sunday service by Mary's own pastor at his own pulpit. Mt. Zion was THE primary Albany Movement mass meeting site (alongside Shiloh Baptist Church across the street, per National Park Service and National Register documentation). The original Pass 1 candidate 'Mount Olive Baptist Church (Albany)' is not supported by external sources: Greater 2nd Mt. Olive in Albany was not founded until 1966 (after these 1961–1962 events); Mount Olive in Sasser (Terrell County, ~30 miles from Albany) was a Sherrod-affiliated SNCC site but had no connection to Rev. James Grant; and Mount Olivet (the church associated with Charles Sherrod) is in Petersburg/Surry, Virginia, where Sherrod grew up. The Whisper rendering 'Molles Island' is a corrupt phonetic transcription of 'Mt. Zion' from elderly speaker diction (Mary Jenkins was 84 at recording).
